entries.current_owner: Swift application and widget shells
entries.target_owner: Swift and future Kotlin platform shells
entries.reason: Composition, native navigation, intents, widgets, and transient presentation are platform responsibilities.
entries.boundary: Dispatch typed application intents and render bounded projections.
entries.persisted_state: Widget app-group snapshot only; never an authoritative domain store.

entries.current_owner: Swift AVFoundation and MediaPlayer adapters
entries.target_owner: Swift AVFoundation and Kotlin Media3 adapters
entries.reason: Audio sessions, routes, media controls, and composition execute OS capabilities.
entries.boundary: Execute typed playback/audio requests and return bounded raw observations.
entries.persisted_state: None; durable playback policy belongs to the shared core.

entries.current_owner: Swift generated-core module integration
entries.target_owner: Swift and future Kotlin projection adapters
entries.reason: Native shells import the generated app-owned facade and translate bounded projections into platform-observable presentation state.
entries.boundary: Use generated facade types directly; do not mirror durable models or make product decisions in the adapter.
entries.persisted_state: None.

entries.current_owner: Swift URLSession and platform credential capability adapters
entries.target_owner: Swift and future Kotlin primitive provider executors
entries.reason: HTTP execution, streamed byte bounds, and secure platform credential access are native capabilities; semantic qualification and durable policy remain in Rust.
entries.boundary: Execute one typed request and return exact bounded bytes, provider envelopes, metadata, and raw typed failures without semantic parsing, retry, fallback, or persistence.
entries.persisted_state: None; credentials remain in platform security facilities and the adapters retain no response state.

entries.current_owner: Swift typed request mapping and transient in-flight task shell
entries.target_owner: Pod0 Rust workflow/application layer issuing typed native host requests
entries.reason: Issue #102 proves the raw provider boundary without activating a second durable writer; issue #100 moves request policy and workflow lifecycle into Rust.
entries.boundary: Consume core-owned bounds, map exact native results into generated observations, call Rust qualification, and retain only bounded in-memory task identities.
entries.persisted_state: None; no files, database rows, retry counters, selected artifacts, or canonical mirrors.
entries.deletion_target: Issue #100 deletes Swift request/workflow orchestration after Rust emits equivalent typed host requests; retain only primitive publisher/model capability execution.

entries.current_owner: Swift provider observation mapper and bounded Rust projection adapter
entries.target_owner: Swift and future Kotlin native capability/projection adapters
entries.reason: Native providers produce platform values and native presentation consumes transcript pages, while Rust owns canonical artifacts, selection, and readiness.
entries.boundary: Map native observations once into generated commands and reconstruct presentation values only from bounded Rust projections.
entries.persisted_state: None; the adapter owns no durable transcript copy or selection policy.

entries.current_owner: Swift design and Apple speech/audio capture
entries.target_owner: Platform-native presentation and capture adapters
entries.reason: Typography, haptics, accessibility, animation, and platform speech/capture remain native.
entries.boundary: Return raw capture/transcription observations; do not decide durable workflow policy.
entries.persisted_state: Transient buffers and OS handles only.

entries.current_owner: Swift local product-signal adapter
entries.target_owner: Platform-native privacy and evaluation adapters
entries.reason: Content-free evaluation observations, local consent controls, and manual OS sharing are native product-validation infrastructure rather than authoritative domain state.
entries.boundary: Accept typed committed outcomes from domain owners; never influence commands, projections, or durable product decisions.
entries.persisted_state: Bounded local signal archive with a random, user-rotatable install identifier.

entries.current_owner: Rust owns migrated listening, note, clip, and selected canonical transcript records; Swift retains presentation mappings, read-only legacy import values, and unmigrated Codable domains
entries.target_owner: Pod0 Rust domain crate
entries.reason: Durable identities, settings, notes, clips, memory, and agent facts are cross-platform product state.
entries.boundary: Stable IDs, versioned schemas, commands, and bounded projections.
entries.persisted_state: Rust SQLite for migrated listening, notes, clips, and transcripts; AppState metadata for unmigrated domains only.
entries.deletion_target: Remove migrated Swift records and Codable ownership after each vertical-slice cutover.

entries.current_owner: Rust listening domain with Swift presentation and read-only legacy import values
entries.target_owner: Pod0 Rust listening domain
entries.reason: Podcast, episode, subscription, feed normalization, and policy are stable cross-platform behavior.
entries.boundary: Native fetch returns bytes/validators; Rust normalizes feeds and owns library projections.
entries.persisted_state: Rust SQLite listening tables; Swift values are replaceable projections and legacy import compatibility.
entries.deletion_target: Delete Swift feed/domain ownership while retaining thin platform fetch/export adapters.

entries.current_owner: Rust is the listening, notes, and clips writer; Swift AppStateStore persists unmigrated domains and applies native projections
entries.target_owner: Rust application actor and versioned Rust SQLite schemas
entries.reason: Durable application state and mutation policy require one cross-platform writer.
entries.boundary: Swift renders projections and dispatches commands; importer reads legacy Swift state once.
entries.persisted_state: Rust listening/note/clip tables plus AppState metadata for unmigrated domains; migrated projections are stripped from Swift persistence.
entries.deletion_target: Delete migrated AppState fields/mutation methods; retain only supported read-only import and native projection adapters.

entries.current_owner: Swift workflow planner, job store, runtime, and artifact repository
entries.target_owner: Pod0 Rust workflow/application layer
entries.reason: Desired state, leases, fencing, retry, recovery, and artifact adoption are durable cross-platform decisions.
entries.boundary: Native executes BGTask, URLSession, notification, and platform provider capabilities.
entries.persisted_state: Versioned workflow and artifact tables plus staged files.
entries.deletion_target: Delete each Swift workflow-kind owner after drain/import/cutover and recovery proof.

entries.current_owner: Swift bounded artifact verification executor
entries.target_owner: Pod0 Rust workflow and artifact application layer
entries.reason: Issue #48 removes visible main-actor stalls without delaying product proof; the typed request/result boundary is explicitly temporary until the workflow slice migrates.
entries.boundary: Native callers submit typed file evidence requests; only verified typed results may reach fenced workflow commits.
entries.persisted_state: None; exact immutable file identities are cached in memory only.
entries.deletion_target: Delete the Swift executor when #60 moves artifact verification and recovery into the shared Rust workflow owner.

entries.current_owner: Rust canonical transcript artifacts, selection, readiness, migration state, and the non-authoritative canonical chapter/ad contract; Swift remains the sole production chapter/ad writer until #104
entries.target_owner: Pod0 Rust transcript/knowledge domain
entries.reason: Normalized segments, speakers, timestamps, chapters, and provenance are durable evidence.
entries.boundary: Provider/native capabilities return raw typed payloads; Rust normalizes and versions artifacts.
entries.persisted_state: Rust SQLite owns canonical transcript artifacts and selections; chapter/ad persistence remains Swift-owned until staged import #99 and atomic cutover #104. The #98 contract persists nothing.
entries.deletion_target: Delete Swift normalized chapter/ad models, parser/selection ownership, and direct writers during #104; retain only native provider observation and presentation adapters linked to #100.

entries.current_owner: Swift provider networking and polling clients
entries.target_owner: Rust workflow/network adapters
entries.reason: Provider request, polling, retry, and recovery semantics are cross-platform and durable.
entries.boundary: Credentials are supplied through explicit secure capabilities; provider state stays in Rust workflows.
entries.persisted_state: External provider IDs and workflow recovery evidence.
entries.deletion_target: Delete Swift cloud provider orchestration after workflow-kind cutover.

entries.current_owner: Swift Speech framework adapter
entries.target_owner: Platform speech capability adapters
entries.reason: Apple Speech is an OS capability; Android will use its own native equivalent.
entries.boundary: Execute a typed request and return raw timed speech observations/failure.
entries.persisted_state: None in the adapter.

entries.current_owner: Swift utility-model request and provider error mapping
entries.target_owner: Pod0 Rust workflow and agent application layers
entries.reason: Utility prompt execution, provider choice, and retry/error semantics drive durable cross-platform workflows.
entries.boundary: A future native adapter may execute a typed provider request, but Rust owns the request policy and resulting workflow state.
entries.persisted_state: None in the client; callers currently commit generated workflow artifacts.
entries.deletion_target: Delete Swift utility request policy when each durable workflow kind moves to the shared core.

entries.current_owner: Swift provider and sqlite-vec capability host
entries.target_owner: Rust-owned retrieval backend or a retained typed platform capability selected by M3 portability evidence
entries.reason: The cutover removes Swift product authority now while preserving iOS velocity; the host stores only reconstructible rows and returns raw observations.
entries.boundary: Consume bounded typed rebuild/retrieval requests and return exact generation/span identities with raw lane ranks; never select citations or expose final recall answers.
entries.persisted_state: A disposable sqlite-vec/FTS capability index rebuilt exclusively from the Rust-selected evidence projection.
entries.deletion_target: Delete this Swift host if the M3 backend proof selects Rust; otherwise reclassify the proven provider/index executor as native by design without expanding its typed boundary.

entries.current_owner: Swift agent tools, schemas, prompts, permissions, generated artifacts, skills, and run logs
entries.target_owner: Pod0 Rust agent/application layer
entries.reason: Tool validation, permission, durable commits, jobs, and generated artifact policy must be identical across platforms.
entries.boundary: Native presents conversations and executes typed platform capabilities.
entries.persisted_state: Agent memories/activity, conversations, scheduled tasks, generated artifacts, and run logs.
entries.deletion_target: Delete Swift tool/workflow policy after shared agent cutover; retain native conversation presentation.

entries.current_owner: Rust owns migrated playback policy; Swift still owns temporary chapter navigation/ad-skip helpers and unmigrated agent feature controllers
entries.target_owner: Pod0 Rust listening and agent application domains
entries.reason: Queue/resume/completion and agent turn/tool decisions are durable cross-platform behavior.
entries.boundary: Swift feature views render projections; AVFoundation and LLM/provider capabilities report raw results.
entries.persisted_state: Rust owns playback resume/played state and staged canonical chapter artifacts. Swift ad-skip suppression is bounded session memory only; agent conversation and turn state remain unmigrated.
entries.deletion_target: Issue #104 deletes Swift chapter target/ad-membership/session policy; issue #60 deletes agent policy while retaining thin native view/capability adapters.

entries.current_owner: Swift product-proof controllers
entries.target_owner: Shared knowledge/workflow policy after interaction validation
entries.reason: Auto-snip, recall intent routing, and persisted chat presentation are still being validated behind typed feature/domain boundaries.
entries.boundary: Commit only through typed store/library interfaces; no new independent durable store.
entries.persisted_state: Clip records commit through the Rust facade; controller state is transient.
entries.deletion_target: Replace transcript-selection and workflow policy as those slices migrate; retain native capture UI and typed clip commands.

entries.current_owner: SwiftUI features and native presentation adapters
entries.target_owner: SwiftUI and future Compose presentation
entries.reason: Views, navigation, formatting, accessibility, settings presentation, capture UI, and transient view models stay native.
entries.boundary: Read projections and dispatch typed intents; do not open durable stores directly.
entries.persisted_state: Transient presentation only; current exceptions are tracked by shared/temporary entries.

entries.current_owner: Swift platform and security services
entries.target_owner: Platform-specific capability adapters
entries.reason: Credential custody, deep links, notifications, Spotlight, native export, widget snapshots, and URLSession delegates execute Apple capabilities.
entries.boundary: Accept typed requests and report raw results; no durable product-policy decisions.
entries.persisted_state: Keychain, iCloud KVS, app-group widget snapshot, and OS-managed transfer handles.

entries.current_owner: Swift product/application services
entries.target_owner: Pod0 Rust domain/application/workflow layers plus thin native capabilities
entries.reason: This mixed service layer currently owns subscription, download, transcript, knowledge, agent, Nostr, and artifact decisions that must migrate by vertical slice.
entries.boundary: Existing typed service protocols are characterization seams; no second store or new untracked policy.
entries.persisted_state: AppState, workflow/artifact SQLite, transcript files, chat history, logs, and generated media.
entries.deletion_target: Delete or reduce each service to a platform capability adapter at its domain cutover.
